revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:鉾持神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Hokochi Shrine |image = File:鉾持神社01.JPG |location = {{ill|Ina, Nagano|en|Ina, Nagano|qid=Q840888|lt=Ina, Nagano|1=Ina, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.840278|N|138.055556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |main deity = {{ill|Izu Gongen|ja|伊豆権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊豆山権現|1=Izusan Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11380566}} (Ninigi-no-Mikoto)
{{ill|Hakone Gongen|ja|箱根権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakone Gongen|qq=}} (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto)
{{ill|Mishima Gongen|ja|三島権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三嶋大社|1=Mishima Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q650772}} (Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto){{Cite book|和書|author=伊那市編|title=伊那市ふるさと百科|publisher=新葉社|year=2007|isbn=978-4-88242-184-9|pages=102}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |established = Traditionally 721 CE (Yōrō 5) |map = Japan Nagano |label_position = bottom |location = {{ill|Ina|qid=Q840888|1=Ina, Nagano}} }} '''Hokochi Shrine''' (鉾持神社, *Hokochi Jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ina, Nagano|en|Ina, Nagano|qid=Q840888|lt=Ina, Nagano|1=Ina, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It holds the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine was traditionally founded in {{ill|721|en|721|qid=Q23541|lt=721|1=721}} (Yōrō 5) by {{ill|Kojihata no Yakumochi|ja|小治田宅持|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojihata no Yakumochi|1=oharida no yakamochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q95162058}}, a governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|qid=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Izu Shrine|ja|伊豆神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Shrine|qq=}} (Ninigi-no-Mikoto), {{ill|Hakone Shrine|en|Hakone Shrine|qid=Q705310|lt=Hakone Shrine|1=Hakone Shrine}} (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto), and {{ill|Mishima Taisha|en|Mishima Taisha|qid=Q650772|lt=Mishima Taisha|1=Mishima Taisha}} (Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto).{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nagareki.com/takatou/hoko.html|title=長野県：歴史・観光・見所|accessdate=2017-01-29}} The shrine was relocated several times before being established at its current site in {{ill|1184|en|1184|qid=Q19728|lt=1184|1=1184}} (Genryaku 1) by Hino Kitayu Muneshige. In {{ill|1185|en|1185|qid=Q19729|lt=1185|1=1185}} (Bunji 1), a sacred spear (*reihoko*) was unearthed from the shrine’s previous location, leading to the current name, Hokochi Shrine ("Shrine of the Sacred Spear"). The current main hall (*honden*) was rebuilt in {{ill|1774|en|1774|qid=Q7697|lt=1774|1=1774}} (An'ei 3), while the *kagura-den* was constructed in {{ill|1907|en|1907|qid=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), and the worship hall (*haiden*) was renovated in {{ill|1915|en|1915|qid=Q2084|lt=1915|1=1915}} (Taishō 4).{{Cite book|和書|author=高遠町誌編纂委員会 |title=高遠町誌 下巻 |publisher=高遠町誌刊行会 |year=1979|page=1269}} The approach to the shrine includes a stone staircase with 321 steps.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.inadanikankou.jp/area/ina/%E9%89%BE%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE|title=鉾持神社|publisher=信州伊那谷観光情報|accessdate=2017-01-29}} == Jūyokka-ichi (Daruma Market) == The annual festival, known as '''Jūyokka-ichi''' (十四日市), or the "Daruma Market," is held on {{ill|February 11|en|February 11|qid=Q2335|lt=February 11|1=February 11}}. Originally a market for exchanging rice seeds and silkworm eggs during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, it evolved into a daruma doll market around the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}. The festival is unique in the Ina Valley for its focus on daruma dolls, especially those believed to bring good fortune.{{Cite book|和書|author=笹本正治 監修 |title=高遠風土記 |publisher=高遠町教育委員会 |year=2004|page=230}} The name "Jūyokka-ichi" originates from the old practice of holding the event on the 14th day of the lunar new year. == Gallery == 鉾持神社社殿.jpg|Hokochi Shrine Main Hall 鉾持神社参道4.jpg|Approach to Hokochi Shrine 鉾持神社参道 001.JPG|Stone Steps of the Shrine Approach だるま、お札、お守り等集積所.jpg|Collection Site for Daruma Dolls and Amulets 小さな祠.jpg|Small Shrine 2017th year 2月11日鉾持神社 幟.jpg|Festival Banners (2017) 2017th year 2月11日 鉾持神社十四日市（だるま市）.jpg|Daruma Market (2017) Hokoji-jinja Shrine Daruma Bazar.jpg|Visitors at the Daruma Market Hokoji-jinja Shrine Daruma Throw.jpg|Daruma Collection Site Filled with Dolls 2017th year 2月11日 鉾持神社十四日市（だるま市）の露天のだるま01.jpg|Daruma Stalls (2017) 2017th year 2月11日 鉾持神社十四日市（だるま市）の露天のだるま02.jpg|Daruma Stalls (2017) == See Also == * {{ill|Ina, Nagano#Attractions|ja|伊那市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina, Nagano#Attractions|1=Ina, Nagano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q840888}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://inashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/contents/archives/27132 Ina City Tourism Association - Hokochi Shrine] (in Japanese) {{Shinto-stub}} {{Nagano-prefecture-stub}} ]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]] ]]]]]] ]]]]]]]]]]]] {{translated page|ja|鉾持神社|version=101735667|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鉾持神社]] {{Commons category|Hokoji-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q21061176}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Daruma]] [[Category:Gongen]] [[Category:History of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
